label: Simple paronychia without abscess or felon
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of simple paronychia or nail-fold infection.
No drainable abscess, felon, flexor tenosynovitis concern, spreading cellulitis, diabetes foot pathway, or immunocompromised-host pathway.
exclusion_criteria: Abscess, pus pocket, felon, flexor tenosynovitis, deep-space hand infection, or procedure-level drainage plan.
Spreading cellulitis, systemic illness, diabetes foot risk, peripheral vascular disease, neuropathy, or immunocompromised host.
Bite wound, crush injury, open fracture, or specialist-directed hand plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving in 1 to 2 days, or sooner if they worsen.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports paronychia framing, nail-fold infection symptoms, pus or abscess possibility, diabetes risk, and clinician escalation for worsening infection. Autonomous Phase 1551-1600 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed simple paronychia without abscess, felon, flexor tenosynovitis, spreading cellulitis, systemic illness, high-risk host factors, bite, crush, open fracture, or specialist hand plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports paronychia framing, nail-fold infection symptoms, pus or abscess possibility, diabetes risk, and clinician escalation for worsening infection. Autonomous Phase 1551-1600 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed simple paronychia without abscess, felon, flexor tenosynovitis, spreading cellulitis, systemic illness, high-risk host factors, bite, crush, open fracture, or specialist hand plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of simple paronychia or nail-fold infection
no abscess, pus pocket, felon, or drainable collection documented
no spreading cellulitis, systemic illness, high-risk host, bite, crush, open fracture, or specialist-directed pathway
